prs.title: [ROCm][Bugfix] Fix init-time bias dtype cast when gate.out_dtype is None
prs.author: rbrugaro-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

Fix a regression on ROCm where the init-time `e_score_correction_bias` dtype cast introduced in #39999 became a no-op after #39141 removed the `set_out_dtype()` call, re-introducing a per-layer elementwise kernel on every forward pass.

## Root Cause

PR #39999 (merged Apr 24) moved the bias dtype conversion from runtime to model construction:

```python
self.gate.e_score_correction_bias.data = (
    self.gate.e_score_correction_bias.data.to(self.gate.out_dtype)
)
```

This relied on `self.gate.out_dtype` being set by an explicit `set_out_dtype()` call that existed immediately above:

```python
self.gate.set_out_dtype(
    torch.float32
    if self.experts.quant_method.is_monolithic
    and self.experts.routing_method_type == RoutingMethodType.DeepSeekV3
    else torch.bfloat16
)
```

Three days later, PR #39141 (commit `c8bbe0518`, Apr 27) removed that `set_out_dtype()` call as part of a TRTLLM routing refactor, leaving `self.gate.out_dtype = None` for all DeepSeek/Kimi-K2 models.

Since `tensor.to(None)` is a no-op in PyTorch, the bias stays fp32 while the gate output (router logits) is bf16. The conditional runtime safety net in `_aiter_ops.py` then fires on every forward pass:

```python
if correction_bias.dtype != gating_output.dtype:
    correction_bias = correction_bias.to(gating_output.dtype)
```

This launches the same elementwise cast kernel per MoE layer that #39999 was designed to eliminate.

## Fix

Fall back to `self.gate.weight.dtype` when `out_dtype` is `None`:

```python
gate_out_dtype = self.gate.out_dtype or self.gate.weight.dtype
```

This is safe because the entire code block is gated by `self.is_rocm_aiter_moe_enabled`, and on ROCm the gate always uses Tier-3 dispatch (F.linear via ReplicatedLinear), where the output dtype equals the weight dtype (bf16). If `out_dtype` is properly set in the future, it takes precedence over the fallback.

## Tests:
Elementwise no longer present in Kimi-

prs.title: [Attention] Abstract the MLA prefill backends and eliminate cuDNN
prs.author: MatthewBonanni
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
Abstracts the MLA prefill backends to simplify `mla_attention.py` and introduces a selection mechanism similar to that of the decode backends, via `--attention-config.mla_prefill_backend`. Old `AttentionConfig` arguments (`use_cudnn_prefill`, `use_trtllm_ragged_deepseek_prefill`, and `disable_flashinfer_prefill`) are retained (with deprecation warnings) for backwards compatibility.

Also eliminates cuDNN for simplicity since this backend is generally not used.

## Test Plan
```
tests/v1/attention/test_mla_prefill_selector.py
```
(introduced by this PR) should pass in CI (part of `V1 attention (H100)` and `V1 attention (B200)`)

## Test Result
TBD

prs.title: [vLLM IR] 2/N fused_add_rms_norm and maybe_inplace overload
prs.author: ProExpertProg
prs.body_head:
## Summary
This PR extends the vLLM IR framework to support optional in-place semantics 
(reusing input activation memory for outputs) via a new `maybe_inplace` overload.
It includes dispatching/registration infrastructure and additional compiler passes
to manage functionalization through the compilation pipeline.
Finally, this PR ports the `fused_add_rms_norm` to IR using the `maybe_inplace` overload.

Key features:
- **`maybe_inplace` overload**: Ops can allow in-place implementations to reuse activation tensor memory
- **eager-mode functionalization**: When the default overload dispatches to an inplace implementation,
activations are cloned to preserve their contents
- **E2E Compilation pipeline integration**: New and existing passes manage IR ops through the compilation pipeline, 
from functionalization to lowering and clone elimination.
- **Fusion passes updated**: Now using the `fused_add_rms_norm` IR op instead of 
- **IR documentation**: Added a full doc on all vLLM IR features: registration, dispatching, compilation, and overall design.
- **Unit & integration tests**: IR infra, kernels, compilation, etc.

## Motivation

vLLM IR separates operation **semantics** from **implementation**, enabling multiple implementations per op.
However, some kernels might optimize memory usage by reusing input activation buffers for outputs.
This poses two problems: (1) torch.compile does not like in-place operations, 
and (2) not all implementations of the IR op will have the same behavior.

To solve this, we introduce the `maybe_inplace` overload, which is allowed (but not required)
to alias output tensors to inputs. When a user calls the `maybe_inplace` overload, it signals
to the dispatcher & compiler that the inputs are no longer needed and their memory may be reused for outputs.

## Changes

### Core IR (`vllm/ir/op.py`)

1. **Extended `@register_op` decorator**: `allow_inplace` and (optional) `activations` parameters
2. **New `maybe_in

prs.title: [ROCm][FEAT] AITER Fused Allreduce + RMSNorm
prs.author: vllmellm
prs.body_head:
## Purpose

This PR integrates AITER fused all-reduce + RMSNorm kernel for ROCm platforms, 

introduces:
-  `RocmAiterAllReduceFusionPass` that fuses the all-reduce collective with the subsequent RMSNorm (and fused-add-RMSNorm) 

AITER's `v0.1.10.post3` version exposes two new function `custom_fused_ar_rms` and `custom_fused_ar_rms_quant` in `CustomAllReduce` class. In this PR we are only utilizing `custom_fused_ar_rms` from AITER's CustomAllReduce only during fusion pass pattern matching. 

###  Changes

in `vllm/_aiter_ops.py`:

- `AiterCustomAllreduceProto`: typed Protocol for the AITER `CustomAllreduce` object 
- `rocm_aiter_ops.initialize_aiter_allreduce(group, device)`: initializes the AITER all-reduce communicator.
- `rocm_aiter_ops.get_aiter_allreduce()` / `destroy_aiter_allreduce()`: lifecycle management
- `torch.ops.vllm.rocm_aiter_fused_allreduce_rmsnorm`: registered custom op with fake impl for `torch.compile`

in (`vllm/compilation/passes/pass_manager.py`):

When `fuse_allreduce_rms` is enabled and `rocm_aiter_ops.is_enabled()` is `True`, `RocmAiterAllReduceFusionPass` is used instead of `AllReduceFusionPass`. CUDA behavior is unchanged.

in (`vllm/config/vllm.py`):

On ROCm, `fuse_allreduce_rms` is now enabled automatically when:
- `VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER=1`
- RMSNorm AITER ops are enabled
- `tensor_parallel_size > 1`

in `vllm/distributed/parallel_state.py`:

The AITER `CustomAllreduce.capture()` context manager is included alongside the existing `ca_comm.capture()` during CUDA graph capture, so the fused kernel is correctly captured.

## Tests

- `tests/compile/passes/distributed/test_fusion_all_reduce.py`: extended to test `RocmAiterAllReduceFusionPass` on ROCm; FP8/FP4 quant fusion tests are skipped on ROCm (not yet supported)
- `tests/compile/fusions_e2e/test_tp2_ar_rms.py`: `test_tp2_ar_rms_fusions` now runs on both CUDA and ROCm with ROCm attention backends (`ROCM_ATTN`, `ROCM_AITER_UNIFIED_ATTN`)
- `.buildk

prs.title: [ROCm] Enable DBO (Dynamic Batch Optimization) on ROCm
prs.author: raviguptaamd
prs.body_head:
This PR enables DBO (`--enable-dbo`) on AMD GPUs by making two minimal changes to `vllm/v1/worker/gpu_ubatch_wrapper.py`:

## Changes

1. **Relax the CUDA-only assertion in `SMControlContextManager`** — The HIP runtime exposes CU (Compute Unit) count via `torch.cuda.get_device_properties().multi_processor_count`, so the existing SM control logic works unchanged on ROCm. The assertion is updated to accept both CUDA and ROCm platforms.

2. **Platform-aware default for `VLLM_DBO_COMM_SMS`** — MI300X has 304 CUs vs H100's 132 SMs, so the CUDA default of 20 is too conservative for ROCm. When the env var is not explicitly set, ROCm defaults to 64 CUs for communication. Users can still override via `VLLM_DBO_COMM_SMS=<N>`.

## Purpose

Enable `--enable-dbo` flag to work on AMD GPUs (currently blocked by a CUDA-only assertion), allowing Dynamic Batch Optimization to overlap communication and computation using CU partitioning on ROCm.

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix and stabilize EAGLE3 acceptance tests
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
This updates the EAGLE3 acceptance length regression test to use ROCm backend auto-selection instead of hard-selecting attention backends. It adds ROCm-specific expected per-position acceptance values for gpt-oss without widening the default tolerance. The Qwen3-VL FP8 MoE case is limited to ROCm TP4 and enables expert parallelism to avoid the unsupported block-FP8 TP shard shape. The per-position check now treats lower-than-baseline acceptance as the regression signal, so improvements do not fail the test. 

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Align spec decode logprob test prefill settings
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
This PR fixes a ROCm-only failure in `test_spec_decode_logprobs` for the ngram speculative decoding cases. The test compares logprobs from a regular base-model run against logprobs from a speculative decoding run. For that comparison to isolate speculative decoding, both LLM instances need to use the same execution settings. Before this change, the spec-decode LLM forced chunked prefill with `enable_chunked_prefill=True` and `max_num_batched_tokens=32`, while the reference LLM did not.

On ROCm, floating-point reductions can vary with batch geometry and scheduling. That means the two runs could produce slightly different logprobs even when speculative decoding itself was behaving correctly. The observed failure was a logprob difference of about `0.235`, which exceeded the test tolerance.

This change factors the shared LLM options into `llm_kwargs` and passes them to both the reference and spec-decode LLMs. The only intended difference between the two runs is now the speculative decoding configuration, so the test better serves its original purpose.

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [Bugfix][CI][Hardware][AMD] Fix various e4m3fn -> e4m3fnuz normalization issues
prs.author: mawong-amd
prs.body_head:
## Purpose
This PR fixes various issues in e4m3fnuz handling encountered by various quantization code paths (ModelOpt, online). In particular, on `e4m3fnuz` platforms, `normalize_e4m3fn_to_e4m3fnuz` should be called exactly once on a given FP8 linear layer after weights are loaded.
However,
- Some PyTorch linear kernel-based code paths used during ModelOpt quantization were not calling this function.
- Some code paths used in online quantization methods were trying to call this function twice, once in the linear layer and once more in the linear layer's kernel implementation.

The end result is that the Quantization test group should now be passing on ROCm.

This PR also removes a Quark mxfp4 model that was causing failures in the Kernels MoE test group because it is incompatible with HF Transformers v5.
## Test Plan
`VLLM_TEST_FORCE_LOAD_FORMAT=auto pytest -v -s quantization/ --ignore quantization/test_blackwell_moe.py`
`pytest -v -s kernels/moe --ignore=kernels/moe/test_modular_oai_triton_moe.py`
These tests should be run as part of the Quantization and Kernels MoE test groups in vLLM AMD CI.

## Test Result
The above test groups pass, and there are no regressions.

cc @kenroche and @AndreasKaratzas

---

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix GPT-OSS Quark MXFP4+FP8 MoE startup
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
Fix GPT-OSS Quark MXFP4+FP8 MoE startup on ROCm/gfx950 by applying the padding required by the Triton/CDNA4 MXFP4 scale layout, and align the GPT-OSS Quark monolithic MoE method with the current `MoERunner` call signature. The GPQA ROCm config for `amd/gpt-oss-20b-MoE-Quant-W-MXFP4-A-FP8-KV-FP8` failed during model startup while processing MoE weights:

```text
RuntimeError: shape '[-1, 90, 2, 16, 11, 2, 4, 1]' is invalid for input of size 8294400
```

The failing path is `QuarkOCP_MX_MoEMethod_OSS`, used for GPT-OSS Quark weights with MXFP4 weights and static FP8 activations. This subclass still swizzles MXFP4 weights and scales through Triton kernels in `process_weights_after_loading()`.

However, the base `QuarkOCP_MX_MoEMethod` treats `w_mxfp4_a_fp8` as an emulation path for sizing purposes, so it does not apply the MXFP4 backend padding. On ROCm/CDNA4, Triton scale swizzling requires aligned dimensions. For the 20B TP=2 case, the unpadded dimensions produce scale shapes based on `hidden_size=2880` and `intermediate_size_per_partition=1440`; `hidden_size / 32 = 90`, which is not compatible with the CDNA4 scale swizzle layout.

## Testing

Ran the focused padded MoE test file:

```text
pytest -s -v evals/gpt_oss/test_gpqa_correctness.py --config-list-file=configs/models-gfx950.txt
```

cc @kenroche

prs.title: [ROCm][CI] Fix NIXL spec-decode acceptance startup and diagnostics
prs.author: AndreasKaratzas
prs.body_head:
- Use `ATTENTION_BACKEND=ROCM_ATTN` override in AMD Buildkite for the NIXL speculative-decoding acceptance test.
- Make the NIXL spec-decode integration script fail fast when a server process exits before readiness instead of polling for the full timeout.
- Pin this integration test path to `127.0.0.1` and set `VLLM_NIXL_SIDE_CHANNEL_HOST` explicitly so HTTP, proxy traffic, and NIXL side-channel metadata use the same loopback address family.
- Improve NIXL KV-cache registration diagnostics when a tensor does not match the expected block dimension, including layer name, shape, stride, backend list, KV layout, and topology mode.

## Scope Notes

1. HTTP readiness is not enough for NIXL. The failing logs showed `/v1/completions` returning 200, then decode later timing out in `_nixl_handshake()` while waiting for prefill metadata on the side-channel socket. The new readiness check sends the same NIXL `get_meta_msg` request over ZMQ that decode needs, so the test does not start until the side channel is actually answering.

2. `localhost` can resolve through IPv6 while the proxy path is already intentionally bound to `127.0.0.1` for CI. This test now uses `127.0.0.1` consistently and passes that host into the Python test for proxy and metrics access.

cc @kenroche
